case_metadata.case_name: Est. of Essex v. Grant County Pub. Hosp. Dist. No. 1
case_metadata.court: Supreme Court of the State of Washington

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Cindy Essex went to Samaritan Hospital’s emergency room complaining of unbearable pain in her left shoulder and chest that radiated to her abdomen.
evidence_index.summary: Cindy Essex presented to the emergency room with severe pain.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Doctors working at, but not as employees of, Samaritan failed to diagnose Cindy’s necrotizing fasciitis, an aggressive soft-tissue infection.
evidence_index.summary: Doctors failed to diagnose necrotizing fasciitis.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Cindy died less than 24 hours later.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Cumbo concluded that had Cindy’s symptoms been “caught sooner... the outcome might have been different.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Cumbo testified that earlier detection could have changed the outcome.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Thomas Cumbo

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We conclude that a hospital cannot escape liability for the negligent provision of emergency services by delegating that duty to its nonemployee doctors.
evidence_index.summary: Hospital cannot avoid liability by delegating duties to nonemployee doctors.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Our statutes and regulations create a nondelegable duty concerning the provision of those services.
evidence_index.summary: Statutes impose nondelegable duty on hospitals for emergency services.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Davis ordered x-rays and a CT (computerized tomography) scan to keep his “diagnostic net fairly wide.”
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Wiest reviewed Cindy’s CT scans from Samaritan and identified “some soft-tissue skin changes” that Dr. Cruite previously failed to recognize and report.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Wiest identified changes missed by Dr. Cruite.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: We reverse the Court of Appeals and remand to the trial court for further proceedings consistent with this opinion.
evidence_index.summary: Court of Appeals decision reversed and remanded.
evidence_index.speaker: Court conclusion

evidence_index.verbatim_quote: Dr. Cumbo took the position that “the hospital was negligent with respect to its oversight and training of its nursing staff.
evidence_index.summary: Dr. Cumbo testified on hospital's negligence in staff oversight.
evidence_index.speaker: Dr. Thomas Cumbo

verdict_detected.key_phrase: We reverse the Court of Appeals and remand to the trial court for further proceedings consistent with this opinion.

initial_state.chief_complaint: Unbearable pain in left shoulder and chest radiating to abdomen
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Cindy Essex presented to the emergency room with severe pain.

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

critical_findings: Severe pain in left shoulder and chest radiating to abdomen

requestables.name: CT Scan
requestables.description: CT scan to evaluate soft tissue and other potential causes of pain

reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt further investigation and treatment

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient in ED with severe pain and imaging findings
decision_points.prompt: What should be the next step in management?

options.description: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics and surgical consultation
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard care for suspected necrotizing fasciitis
Failed to address potential life-threatening infection

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant continued observation and pain management

expected_action_court.description: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics and surgical consultation
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established need for immediate intervention

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Based on evidence E002 and E004
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Immediate intervention with antibiotics and surgical consultation

timeline_phases.name: Emergency Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents to ED with acute symptoms

key_events.event: Patient arrives at ED with severe pain

patient_outcome.description: Patient died less than 24 hours later due to necrotizing fasciitis

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to diagnose and treat necrotizing fasciitis
Causation established - earlier intervention could have changed the outcome

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to diagnose and treat necrotizing fasciitis
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Earlier intervention could have changed the outcome

factual_timeline.event: Patient arrives at ED with severe pain

allegations.allegation: Failure to diagnose and treat necrotizing fasciitis
